draft.introduction: A psalm of spiritual longing attributed to the Sons of Korah. The image of the deer thirsting for water became one of the most enduring metaphors for the soul’s desire for God.

translation.text: As a deer pants for flowing streams, so pants my soul for you, O God. My soul thirsts for God, for the living God. When shall I come and appear before God? My tears have been my food day and night.
translation.original_text: ὃν τρόπον ἐπιποθεῖ ἡ ἔλαφος ἐπὶ τὰς πηγὰς τῶν ὑδάτων

translator_notes.note: The LXX uses ἐπιποθεῖ (“longs for, yearns”), a strong verb of desire. Augustine’s commentary on this psalm became foundational for Western mystical theology.
